prdTitle: Call-Out Management, Timecard Locking & External Availability

epics.title: Call-Out Management, Timecard Locking & External Availability
epics.description: Extend MaxView's existing RTO capability into a broader call-out and attendance management system. This initiative adds structured call-out classification, automatic timecard-day locking for approved absences, paid-sick-time payout logic with accrual visibility, contract-position backfill shift creation, and self-service availability management for External Users.
successMetrics: ≥ 90% of call-outs entered through structured flow within 60 days of launch
Reduce timecard corrections filed post-absence by ≥ 80%
100% of paid-time-off selections made with visible accrual balance
≥ 75% of eligible contract-position call-outs auto-create backfill shifts
≥ 50% of active External Users with a saved general schedule within 90 days
outOfScope: Negative sick-time accommodation (explicitly rejected per stakeholder direction)
Changes to existing RTO cancellation/replacement-shift logic already live
Credential or onboarding workflows
Mobile-app surfaces (future phase)
Payroll integration beyond surfacing accrual and marking paid/unpaid

features.title: Wave 1 — Call-Out & Timecard Foundation
features.description: Establishes all foundational infrastructure that Waves 2–4 depend on: RtoRequest entity extensions for call-out classification, WorksiteCallOutSetting for configurable lead-time thresholds, timecard day-level lock mechanism, and feature flag registration. No user-visible behavior is delivered in this wave.
outOfScope: Any user-facing route, UI component, or notification
Accrual, backfill, or availability features

slices.title: Call-out & classification schema foundation — RtoRequest extensions, WorksiteCallOutSetting, feature flag
slices.sliceRationale: Foundational TBI enabling all Wave 2–4 call-out slices. Extends the RtoRequest entity with call-out type classification, creates the worksite-level threshold configuration table, and registers the feature flag. No user-visible path — kept as standalone TBI because it enables multiple downstream PBI slices across three waves.
slices.description: DB migration extending RtoRequest with CallOutType enum (Late, LeaveEarly, FullDay), IsCallOut boolean flag, and classification metadata. Creates WorksiteCallOutSetting table (WorksiteId, ThresholdHours) for worksite-level lead-time thresholds. Registers FF_CallOutManagement feature flag in FeatureFlag table. All new columns are nullable to preserve backward compatibility with existing RTO requests.
nonFunctionalRequirements: Migration must be idempotent and roll back cleanly
New nullable columns must not break existing RTO queries or consumers
definitionOfDone: EF migration applies cleanly to dev database and rolls back cleanly in a test
CallOutType enum is defined in RtoRequest entity model with values Late, LeaveEarly, FullDay
WorksiteCallOutSetting entity is registered in EF DbContext with WorksiteId + ThresholdHours columns
FF_CallOutManagement feature flag is seeded via migration data
Existing RTO unit and integration tests pass without modification

adoFields.System.Title: Call-out & classification schema foundation — RtoRequest extensions, WorksiteCallOutSetting, feature flag
adoFields.System.Description:
DB migration extending RtoRequest with CallOutType enum (Late, LeaveEarly, FullDay), IsCallOut boolean flag, and classification metadata. Creates WorksiteCallOutSetting table (WorksiteId, ThresholdHours) for worksite-level lead-time thresholds. Registers FF_CallOutManagement feature flag in FeatureFlag table. All new columns are nullable to preserve backward compatibility with existing RTO requests.

**Slice rationale:** Foundational infrastructure enabling all Wave 2–4 call-out slices. No user-visible path — kept as standalone TBI because it enables multiple downstream PBI slices.

**Recomposed from:** TBI-001, TBI-002, TBI-009

**Definition of Done:**
- [ ] EF migration applies cleanly to dev database and rolls back cleanly in a test
- [ ] CallOutType enum defined in RtoRequest entity model with values Late, LeaveEarly, FullDay
- [ ] WorksiteCallOutSetting entity registered in EF DbContext with WorksiteId + ThresholdHours
- [ ] FF_CallOutManagement feature flag seeded via migration data
- [ ] Existing RTO unit and integration tests pass without modification

slices.title: Timecard day-level lock infrastructure — per-entry-date lock with reason
slices.sliceRationale: Foundational TBI enabling the Wave 2 timecard-day locking PBI. The current Timecard system only locks entire timecards by status (InProgress → Submitted → Approved). This TBI adds a new day-level lock mechanism so individual entry dates can be disabled independently. No user-visible path on its own.
slices.description: Build day-level locking mechanism for Timecards. The current system only supports locking entire Timecards by status — there is no per-day lock concept. This TBI adds a structure (table or per-entry flag) that stores lock status + reason per day per Timecard. TimecardOperation and TimeCardOperations expose a lock-check method returning isLocked + reason for a given HCP + date combination.
nonFunctionalRequirements: Lock-check query must not add measurable latency to Timecard page load (< 5 ms additional)
Must not break existing Timecard status-based locking or approval flow
definitionOfDone: Day-level lock structure created (table or per-entry flag) and migration applies cleanly
TimecardOperation exposes a method to check lock status for a given HCP + date
Lock check returns reason string when locked (e.g., 'Approved RTO: Full Day') and false when unlocked
Existing Timecard unit and integration tests pass without modification
Unit test covers: locked day returns true + reason, unlocked day returns false

adoFields.System.Title: Timecard day-level lock infrastructure — per-entry-date lock with reason
adoFields.System.Description:
Build day-level locking mechanism for Timecards. The current system only supports locking entire Timecards by status (InProgress → Submitted → Approved) — there is no per-day lock concept. This TBI adds a structure that stores lock status + reason per day per Timecard. TimecardOperation and TimeCardOperations expose a lock-check method returning isLocked + reason for a given HCP + date.

**Slice rationale:** Foundational infrastructure enabling Wave 2 timecard-day locking PBI. No user-visible path — the current system has no day-level concept.

**Recomposed from:** TBI-004

**Definition of Done:**
- [ ] Day-level lock structure created and migration applies cleanly
- [ ] TimecardOperation exposes lock-check method for HCP + date
- [ ] Lock check returns reason string when locked, false when unlocked
- [ ] Existing Timecard tests pass without modification
- [ ] Unit test covers locked and unlocked scenarios

adoFields.System.Title: Wave 1 — Call-Out & Timecard Foundation
adoFields.System.Description:
Establishes all foundational infrastructure for call-out classification and timecard day locking. No user-visible behavior is delivered — this wave is a prerequisite for Waves 2–4.

**PRD user stories advanced:** Enables #1 (call-out recording), #2 (auto-classification), #3 (timecard locking)

**Slices in this wave (all parallel):**
- WI-001 (TBI, AFK): Call-out & classification schema foundation — RtoRequest extensions, WorksiteCallOutSetting, feature flag
- WI-002 (TBI, AFK): Timecard day-level lock infrastructure — per-entry-date lock with reason

**No blockers — can start immediately.**

features.title: Wave 2 — Core Call-Out Recording & Timecard Locking
features.description: Delivers two independently demoable user-facing flows: structured call-out recording with auto-classification, and automatic timecard-day locking for approved absences. Both cover the full vertical path from database through API to UI. These are the core operational improvements addressing the primary pain points identified in the PRD.
outOfScope: Paid/unpaid time-off selection or accrual display
Contract-position backfill shift creation
External availability management

slices.title: End-to-end structured call-out recording with auto-classification
slices.sliceRationale: Vertical tracer bullet covering the full call-out recording path: Internal User selects call-out type on both TimeClock Web and MaxHub Mobile RTO forms → lead-time classification engine evaluates WorksiteCallOutSetting → RtoRequest stored with CallOutType + IsCallOut → call-out-specific email notification sent to owners. Recomposed from PBI-001 (type selection), PBI-002 (auto-classification), TBI-003 (engine), and TBI-008 (notification template) into a single demoable unit.

userStory.iWant: record a call-out with a structured type (Late, Leave Early, Full Day) and have the system auto-classify it as a call-out vs planned absence based on the worksite's lead-time threshold
userStory.soThat: call-outs are tracked with consistent categories for reporting and downstream processing without relying on free-text descriptions

businessRules: BR-010: Partial vs full day classification

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User is on the RTO/call-out submission form and the HCP's worksite has a ThresholdHours of 24
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User selects 'Full Day' as the call-out type and submits the request 4 hours before the shift
acceptanceCriteria.then: the RtoRequest is saved with CallOutType = FullDay and IsCallOut = true, a call-out-specific email notification is sent to the CandidateOwner and PositionOwner, and the call-out appears in the RTO list with a call-out indicator

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User is on the RTO submission form and the HCP's worksite has a ThresholdHours of 24
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User submits a time-off request 48 hours before the shift
acceptanceCriteria.then: the RtoRequest is saved with IsCallOut = false (classified as planned absence), no call-out-specific notification is sent, and the request appears in the RTO list without a call-out indicator

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User is on the call-out submission form
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User attempts to submit without selecting a call-out type
acceptanceCriteria.then: the form displays a validation error requiring call-out type selection and the request is not saved

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User submits a call-out exactly at the ThresholdHours boundary (e.g., exactly 24 hours before the shift when threshold is 24)
acceptanceCriteria.when: the lead-time classification engine evaluates the submission
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request is classified as a planned absence (>= threshold) — the boundary is inclusive for planned absence

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User is logged into MaxView
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User attempts to submit a call-out on behalf of a different HCP
acceptanceCriteria.then: the system rejects the request — External Users can only submit RTO for their own Match

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Call-out submission (including classification engine evaluation) must complete within 2 seconds under normal load
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: Call-out type dropdown must be keyboard-navigable and carry ARIA labels describing each option
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: Only authenticated Internal Users and the HCP themselves may submit call-outs; authorization enforced at API layer

outOfScope: Paid/unpaid time-off selection (Wave 3)
Backfill shift creation triggered by the call-out (Wave 3)
Admin UI for managing WorksiteCallOutSetting threshold values (seeded via migration)

adoFields.System.Title: End-to-end structured call-out recording with auto-classification
adoFields.System.Description:
As an Internal User, I want to record a call-out with a structured type (Late, Leave Early, Full Day) and have the system auto-classify it as a call-out vs planned absence based on the worksite's lead-time threshold, so that call-outs are tracked with consistent categories for reporting and downstream processing without relying on free-text descriptions.

**Slice rationale:** Vertical tracer bullet covering call-out recording through both TimeClock Web (RtoOrchestrationService → RtoRequestService) and MaxHub Mobile (AbsenceManagementOperation) paths. Classification engine, email notification, and UI indicator are all included in this single demoable unit.

**Business Rules:** BR-001: Call-out type classification, BR-002: Lead-time classification threshold, BR-010: Partial vs full day classification, BR-012: Call-out notifications

**Acceptance Criteria:**

Given an Internal User is on the RTO/call-out submission form and the HCP's worksite has a ThresholdHours of 24
When the Internal User selects 'Full Day' as the call-out type and submits the request 4 hours before the shift
Then the RtoRequest is saved with CallOutType = FullDay and IsCallOut = true, a call-out-specific email notification is sent, and the call-out appears with an indicator

Given an Internal User submits a time-off request 48 hours before the shift
When the lead-time engine evaluates the submission
Then IsCallOut = false (planned absence), no call-out notification sent

Given an Internal User attempts to submit without selecting a call-out type
When the form is submitted
Then a validation error is displayed and the request is not saved

Given a submission exactly at the ThresholdHours boundary
When the classification engine evaluates
Then the request is classified as planned absence (>= threshold is inclusive)

Given an External User attempts to submit a call-out for a different HCP
When the API processes the request
Then the system rejects it — External Users can only submit for their own Match
adoFields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Given an Internal User is on the RTO/call-out submission form and the HCP's worksite has a ThresholdHours of 24
When the Internal User selects 'Full Day' and submits 4 hours before the shift
Then RtoRequest saved with CallOutType = FullDay, IsCallOut = true, call-out notification sent, indicator shown in list

Given an Internal User submits a time-off request 48 hours before the shift
When the lead-time engine evaluates
Then IsCallOut = false, no call-out notification sent

Given an Internal User attempts to submit without selecting a call-out type
When the form is submitted
Then validation error displayed, request not saved

Given a submission exactly at the ThresholdHours boundary (24 hours, threshold 24)
When classification engine evaluates
Then classified as planned absence (>= threshold is inclusive)

Given an External User attempts to submit a call-out for a different HCP
When the API processes the request
Then the system rejects it — External Users can only submit for their own Match

slices.title: End-to-end timecard-day locking for approved absences
slices.sliceRationale: Vertical tracer bullet from RtoRequest status check through TimeCardController/TimeCardOperations API response to the Timecard UI rendering a grayed-out day. Covers the full path: query RtoRequest per entry date → return isLocked + reason via API → UI renders disabled day with auto-populated absence note → manual entry blocked → unlocked on rescind. Recomposed from PBI-003 (UI locking) and TBI-004 (lock mechanism infrastructure provided by WI-002).

userStory.iWant: my Timecard day to be automatically locked and grayed out when I have an approved RTO or call-out covering that day
userStory.soThat: I cannot accidentally enter time for a day I am absent, eliminating post-absence timecard corrections

businessRules: BR-003: Timecard-day locking on approved absence

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User has an approved full-day RTO for Wednesday
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User opens their Timecard for the week containing that Wednesday
acceptanceCriteria.then: Wednesday is grayed out with an auto-populated note 'Approved RTO: Full Day', the time-entry fields for that day are disabled, and the user cannot submit time for Wednesday

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User's Timecard day is locked due to an approved RTO
acceptanceCriteria.when: an API request attempts to submit a Timesheet line entry for that locked day (bypassing the UI)
acceptanceCriteria.then: the API returns a 400 Bad Request with an explanation that the day is locked due to an approved absence

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User has an approved partial-day absence (Late — arriving 2 hours late)
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User opens their Timecard for that day
acceptanceCriteria.then: the entire day is locked with an auto-populated note 'Approved Call-Out: Late' — partial-day absences lock the full day

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User had an approved RTO for Thursday but the absence was rescinded
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User opens their Timecard for the week containing that Thursday
acceptanceCriteria.then: Thursday is fully editable with no lock indicator — rescinding the absence removes the day-level lock

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Day-level lock check must not add more than 50 ms to Timecard page load time
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: Locked days must have an aria-disabled attribute and a tooltip or aria-label explaining why the day is locked
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: Lock enforcement must occur at the API layer — UI-only disabling is insufficient

outOfScope: Partial-day locking (locking only the affected time window rather than the full day)
Timecard approval workflow changes
Contact-initiated time entry for locked days

adoFields.System.Title: End-to-end timecard-day locking for approved absences
adoFields.System.Description:
As an External User, I want my Timecard day to be automatically locked and grayed out when I have an approved RTO or call-out covering that day, so that I cannot accidentally enter time for a day I am absent, eliminating post-absence timecard corrections.

**Slice rationale:** Vertical tracer bullet from RtoRequest status check through TimeCardController API to Timecard UI gray-out. Covers lock check, API enforcement, UI rendering, and rescind-unlock. Uses the day-level lock infrastructure from WI-002.

**Business Rules:** BR-003: Timecard-day locking on approved absence

**Acceptance Criteria:**

Given an External User has an approved full-day RTO for Wednesday
When the External User opens their Timecard for that week
Then Wednesday is grayed out with note 'Approved RTO: Full Day', time entry disabled

Given a Timecard day is locked due to approved RTO
When an API request attempts to submit a Timesheet line entry for that day
Then the API returns 400 with explanation that the day is locked

Given an External User has an approved partial-day absence (Late)
When the External User opens their Timecard
Then the entire day is locked with note 'Approved Call-Out: Late'

Given an approved RTO for Thursday was rescinded
When the External User opens their Timecard
Then Thursday is fully editable with no lock indicator
adoFields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Given an External User has an approved full-day RTO for Wednesday
When the External User opens their Timecard
Then Wednesday is grayed out with note 'Approved RTO: Full Day', time entry disabled

Given a Timecard day is locked due to approved RTO
When an API request submits a Timesheet line entry for that day
Then API returns 400 with locked-day explanation

Given an approved partial-day absence (Late)
When the External User opens their Timecard
Then the entire day is locked with note 'Approved Call-Out: Late'

Given an RTO for Thursday was rescinded
When the External User opens their Timecard
Then Thursday is fully editable, no lock indicator

adoFields.System.Title: Wave 2 — Core Call-Out Recording & Timecard Locking
adoFields.System.Description:
Delivers two core user-facing flows: structured call-out recording with auto-classification, and automatic timecard-day locking for approved absences. Both are full vertical slices from database through API to UI. Blocked by Wave 1 (foundation).

**PRD user stories advanced:** #1 (call-out recording), #2 (auto-classification), #3 (timecard locking)

**Slices in this wave (all parallel):**
- WI-003 (PBI, AFK): End-to-end structured call-out recording with auto-classification
- WI-004 (PBI, AFK): End-to-end timecard-day locking for approved absences

**Blocked by:** Wave 1 — Call-Out & Timecard Foundation must be fully merged.

features.title: Wave 3 — Paid Time Off & Contract Backfill
features.description: Delivers two independently demoable flows: paid/unpaid time-off selection with real-time accrual balance display, and automatic backfill shift creation for contract-position HCPs. Both extend the call-out submission flow from Wave 2. The accrual slice requires human architectural review (HITL) due to open questions about the RC accrual table structure.
outOfScope: Admin UI for worksite call-out threshold management
Accrual balance write-back to RC payroll
External availability management

slices.title: End-to-end paid/unpaid time-off selection with accrual balance display
slices.sliceRationale: Vertical tracer bullet from RC accrual tables through a new MaxHub API endpoint, MaxView caching layer, RtoRequestService validation, to the UI balance display and paid/unpaid selector. Marked HITL because the RC accrual table structure is an open question (PRD Open Question #2) requiring architectural review before implementation. Recomposed from PBI-004 (display), PBI-005 (selection + guard), and TBI-005 (MaxHub endpoint + cache).

userStory.iWant: see the HCP's current PTO/sick-time accrual balance at RTO submission and select paid or unpaid time off with a guard preventing negative accrual
userStory.soThat: I can make an informed decision without checking an external system and the organization never pays out more sick time than accrued

businessRules: BR-004: Accrual balance visibility at submission
BR-005: No negative accrual — block if zero balance and paid selected

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User is submitting an RTO and the HCP has 24 hours of accrued sick time
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User opens the RTO submission form
acceptanceCriteria.then: the current accrual balance (24 hours) is displayed, the Internal User can select 'Paid Time Off', and upon submission the balance is deducted appropriately

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User is submitting an RTO and the HCP has 0 hours of accrued sick time
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User selects 'Paid Time Off' and attempts to submit
acceptanceCriteria.then: submission is blocked with a clear message: 'Insufficient accrual balance. Current balance: 0 hours. Select Unpaid Time Off to proceed.'

acceptanceCriteria.given: an HCP has exactly 8 hours of accrued sick time and is requesting a full-day (8-hour) absence
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User selects 'Paid Time Off' and submits
acceptanceCriteria.then: submission succeeds — the balance becomes exactly 0 hours, which is permitted

acceptanceCriteria.given: two concurrent RTO submissions for the same HCP with 8 hours of accrual, each requesting 8 hours paid
acceptanceCriteria.when: both submissions are processed
acceptanceCriteria.then: only the first submission succeeds as paid; the second is blocked with an insufficient balance message — the system never allows negative accrual regardless of race conditions

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Accrual balance fetch (including MaxHub API call and cache check) must complete within 2 seconds
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: Accrual balance display and paid/unpaid selector must be keyboard-navigable with ARIA labels
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: Accrual balance is sensitive PII — only the HCP themselves and Internal Users with appropriate permissions may view it
nonFunctionalRequirements.compliance: No accrual data may be cached beyond the user's session lifetime

outOfScope: Writing back accrual deductions to RC payroll system
Accrual balance history or audit trail UI
Configuring accrual rates or policies

adoFields.System.Title: End-to-end paid/unpaid time-off selection with accrual balance display
adoFields.System.Description:
As an Internal User, I want to see the HCP's current PTO/sick-time accrual balance at RTO submission and select paid or unpaid time off with a guard preventing negative accrual, so that I can make an informed decision without checking an external system and the organization never pays out more sick time than accrued.

**Slice rationale:** Vertical tracer bullet from RC accrual tables → MaxHub API endpoint → MaxView cache → RtoRequestService validation → UI balance display + paid/unpaid selector. HITL: RC accrual table structure is an open question requiring architectural review.

**Business Rules:** BR-004: Accrual balance visibility at submission, BR-005: No negative accrual

**Acceptance Criteria:**

Given an HCP has 24 hours of accrued sick time
When the Internal User opens the RTO submission form
Then the balance is displayed and paid selection succeeds with deduction

Given an HCP has 0 hours accrued and Internal User selects Paid
When the form is submitted
Then submission is blocked with insufficient balance message

Given an HCP has exactly 8 hours and requests 8-hour absence as Paid
When the form is submitted
Then submission succeeds — balance becomes exactly 0

Given two concurrent paid submissions for the same HCP with 8 hours
When both are processed
Then only the first succeeds; the second is blocked — no negative accrual
adoFields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Given an HCP has 24 hours of accrued sick time
When the Internal User opens the RTO submission form
Then balance is displayed, paid selection succeeds with deduction

Given an HCP has 0 hours and Internal User selects Paid
When submitted
Then blocked with insufficient balance message

Given exactly 8 hours accrued, requesting 8-hour paid absence
When submitted
Then succeeds — balance becomes 0

Given two concurrent paid submissions, 8 hours available
When both processed
Then only first succeeds; second blocked — no negative accrual

slices.title: End-to-end contract-position backfill shift creation on call-out
slices.sliceRationale: Vertical tracer bullet from position-type detection in RtoRequestService through MaxHub shift creation to the MassTransit consumer mapping the shift back to the RTO request. Today, only substitute HCPs with existing RC per-diem shifts are backfilled; this slice adds the 'create then backfill' path for contract-position HCPs who have no pre-existing shift. Recomposed from PBI-006 (user-visible backfill status) and TBI-006 (contract branching logic).

userStory.iWant: the system to automatically create a backfill shift when a contract-position HCP calls out so that the open shift is available for reassignment
userStory.soThat: I do not have to manually create shifts in RC before initiating backfill for contract HCPs, reducing operational overhead for the majority of call-outs

businessRules: BR-011: Backfill gated by facility setting

acceptanceCriteria.given: a contract-position HCP calls out, the facility setting 'Upon RTO Request Open Shift' is 'yes', and the HCP has no pre-existing RC per-diem shift for that day
acceptanceCriteria.when: the call-out is processed by RtoRequestService
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new per-diem shift is created in RC via MaxHub with attributes sourced from the Match/Position, the shift is available for backfill assignment, and SavePerDiemShiftMapRcShiftConsumer maps it back to the RTO request

acceptanceCriteria.given: a contract-position HCP calls out and the MaxHub shift creation API call fails
acceptanceCriteria.when: the call-out is processed
acceptanceCriteria.then: the RTO request is still processed successfully, no backfill shift is created, the failure is logged, and the Internal User is notified that manual backfill is required

acceptanceCriteria.given: the facility setting 'Upon RTO Request Open Shift' is 'no'
acceptanceCriteria.when: a contract-position HCP calls out
acceptanceCriteria.then: no backfill shift is created regardless of position type — the facility setting gates all backfill behavior

acceptanceCriteria.given: a substitute HCP with an existing RC per-diem shift calls out
acceptanceCriteria.when: the call-out is processed
acceptanceCriteria.then: the existing RC shift is reopened/reassigned using the current substitute backfill flow — no new shift is created (existing behavior preserved)

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Shift creation via MaxHub must complete within 5 seconds; call-out processing must not block on shift creation failure
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: N/A — backfill is a server-side process
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: Shift creation must use the existing authenticated MaxHub API service channel

outOfScope: Modifying the existing substitute backfill flow
Admin UI for managing the 'Upon RTO Request Open Shift' facility setting
Backfill assignment or scheduling beyond shift creation

adoFields.System.Title: End-to-end contract-position backfill shift creation on call-out
adoFields.System.Description:
As an Internal User, I want the system to automatically create a backfill shift when a contract-position HCP calls out so that the open shift is available for reassignment, so that I do not have to manually create shifts in RC before initiating backfill for contract HCPs, reducing operational overhead for the majority of call-outs.

**Slice rationale:** Vertical tracer bullet from position-type detection through MaxHub shift creation to MassTransit consumer. Adds contract-position branching to the existing per-diem-only backfill path.

**Business Rules:** BR-006: Contract-position backfill, BR-007: Substitute backfill unchanged, BR-011: Facility setting gate

**Acceptance Criteria:**

Given a contract-position HCP calls out, facility setting is 'yes', no pre-existing shift
When the call-out is processed
Then a new per-diem shift is created in RC, available for backfill, mapped to the RTO request

Given a contract-position HCP calls out and MaxHub shift creation fails
When the call-out is processed
Then RTO succeeds, no backfill shift, failure logged, Internal User notified

Given the facility setting 'Upon RTO Request Open Shift' is 'no'
When a contract-position HCP calls out
Then no backfill shift created regardless of position type

Given a substitute HCP with existing RC shift calls out
When the call-out is processed
Then existing shift is reopened/reassigned — no new shift created
adoFields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Given a contract-position HCP calls out, facility setting 'yes', no pre-existing shift
When processed
Then new per-diem shift created in RC, available for backfill, mapped to RTO

Given MaxHub shift creation fails
When processed
Then RTO succeeds, no backfill, failure logged, Internal User notified

Given facility setting is 'no'
When contract HCP calls out
Then no backfill shift created

Given substitute HCP with existing RC shift
When processed
Then existing shift reopened — no new shift created

adoFields.System.Title: Wave 3 — Paid Time Off & Contract Backfill
adoFields.System.Description:
Delivers paid/unpaid time-off selection with real-time accrual balance display, and automatic backfill shift creation for contract-position HCPs. Both extend the call-out submission flow from Wave 2. Blocked by Wave 2.

**PRD user stories advanced:** #4 (accrual display), #5 (paid/unpaid selection), #6 (contract backfill)

**Slices in this wave (all parallel):**
- WI-005 (PBI, HITL): End-to-end paid/unpaid time-off selection with accrual balance display
- WI-006 (PBI, AFK): End-to-end contract-position backfill shift creation on call-out

**Blocked by:** Wave 2 — Core Call-Out Recording & Timecard Locking must be fully merged.

features.title: Wave 4 — External Availability
features.description: Delivers self-service availability management for External Users: a recurring general schedule and one-time date-range overrides. This is a net-new capability with no dependency on the call-out or timecard features from Waves 2–3 — it depends only on Wave 1 for the feature flag infrastructure pattern. Gated behind a separate feature flag (FF_CallOutManagement_Availability) for independent rollout.
outOfScope: Internal User self-service schedule (External Users only)
Integration with shift scheduling or per-diem shift matching
Mobile-app availability surfaces

slices.title: End-to-end general schedule availability management for External Users
slices.sliceRationale: Vertical tracer bullet from HcpAvailability table creation through a new AvailabilityController with CRUD endpoints to a self-service UI form. Entirely greenfield — no existing availability code exists in the codebase. This slice owns the schema, API, and UI for recurring weekly availability. WI-008 (overrides) depends on this slice's schema.

userStory.iWant: set my recurring weekly availability (days of week and time-of-day preference) in MaxView
userStory.soThat: my internal team knows when I am generally available for shifts without needing to call or email me

businessRules: BR-008: General schedule availability — days of week + time-of-day preference, editable at any time

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User navigates to the availability section in MaxView
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User selects Mon, Wed, Fri with 'Daytime' preference and saves
acceptanceCriteria.then: the general schedule is persisted in HcpAvailability, the UI confirms the save, and Internal Users can view the External User's availability

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User is on the availability form
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User attempts to save without selecting any days
acceptanceCriteria.then: a validation error is displayed requiring at least one day to be selected

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User already has a saved general schedule (Mon/Wed/Fri Daytime)
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User updates it to Tue/Thu Evening and saves
acceptanceCriteria.then: the previous schedule is fully replaced by the new schedule — no merge, no history

acceptanceCriteria.given: an Internal User views an External User's general schedule
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Internal User attempts to edit the External User's self-service schedule
acceptanceCriteria.then: the edit controls are not available — Internal Users can view but not modify External User self-service availability

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Availability CRUD operations must complete within 1 second
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: Day-of-week checkboxes and time preference selector must be keyboard-navigable with ARIA labels
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: External Users can only view and edit their own availability; Internal Users have read-only access

outOfScope: One-time availability overrides (WI-008)
Integration with shift scheduling or auto-matching
Availability history or audit trail

adoFields.System.Title: End-to-end general schedule availability management for External Users
adoFields.System.Description:
As an External User, I want to set my recurring weekly availability (days of week and time-of-day preference) in MaxView, so that my internal team knows when I am generally available for shifts without needing to call or email me.

**Slice rationale:** Vertical tracer bullet from HcpAvailability table through AvailabilityController CRUD to self-service UI. Entirely greenfield — no existing availability code in the codebase.

**Business Rules:** BR-008: General schedule availability

**Acceptance Criteria:**

Given an External User navigates to the availability section
When Mon/Wed/Fri Daytime is selected and saved
Then schedule is persisted, UI confirms, Internal Users can view it

Given an External User attempts to save without selecting any days
When the form is submitted
Then validation error requiring at least one day

Given an existing schedule Mon/Wed/Fri Daytime
When updated to Tue/Thu Evening
Then previous schedule is fully replaced

Given an Internal User views an External User's schedule
When the Internal User attempts to edit
Then edit controls are not available — read-only access
adoFields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Given an External User navigates to availability section
When Mon/Wed/Fri Daytime selected and saved
Then persisted, confirmed, viewable by Internal Users

Given no days selected
When submitted
Then validation error

Given existing schedule Mon/Wed/Fri Daytime
When updated to Tue/Thu Evening
Then fully replaced

Given Internal User views External User's schedule
When attempts to edit
Then read-only — no edit controls

slices.title: End-to-end one-time availability overrides for External Users
slices.sliceRationale: Vertical tracer bullet from HcpAvailabilityOverride table through AvailabilityController override endpoints to a date-range picker UI plus a background auto-expire job. Depends on WI-007's HcpAvailability schema (intra-wave dependency) because overrides supersede the general schedule. Recomposed from PBI-008 (override UI) and the override portion of TBI-007 (table + API).

userStory.iWant: set one-time availability changes for specific date ranges that override my general schedule
userStory.soThat: I can communicate temporary schedule changes without altering my recurring availability

businessRules: BR-009: One-time overrides supersede general schedule and auto-expire after dates pass

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User has a general schedule of Mon/Wed/Fri and navigates to the override section
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User adds an override for next Tuesday and Thursday as 'Available, Daytime' and saves
acceptanceCriteria.then: the overrides are persisted in HcpAvailabilityOverride, displayed alongside the general schedule, and Internal Users see the combined availability including the overrides

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User is creating an availability override
acceptanceCriteria.when: the External User sets an end date before the start date
acceptanceCriteria.then: a validation error is displayed and the override is not saved

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User has an override marking Wednesday as 'Unavailable' and their general schedule includes Wednesday
acceptanceCriteria.when: Internal Users view the External User's availability for that week
acceptanceCriteria.then: the override takes precedence — Wednesday shows as unavailable despite the general schedule

acceptanceCriteria.given: an External User created overrides for last week that have passed
acceptanceCriteria.when: the auto-expire background job runs
acceptanceCriteria.then: the expired overrides are removed from the active list and are no longer visible to Internal Users or the External User

nonFunctionalRequirements.performance: Override CRUD operations must complete within 1 second; auto-expire job must process all expired overrides within 60 seconds
nonFunctionalRequirements.accessibility: Date-range picker must be keyboard-navigable and announce selected dates via screen reader
nonFunctionalRequirements.security: External Users can only manage their own overrides

outOfScope: Bulk override creation for multiple HCPs
Override approval workflow
Integration with shift scheduling

adoFields.System.Title: End-to-end one-time availability overrides for External Users
adoFields.System.Description:
As an External User, I want to set one-time availability changes for specific date ranges that override my general schedule, so that I can communicate temporary schedule changes without altering my recurring availability.

**Slice rationale:** Vertical tracer bullet from HcpAvailabilityOverride table through AvailabilityController to date-range picker UI + auto-expire background job. Intra-wave dependency on WI-007 (general schedule schema).

**Business Rules:** BR-009: One-time overrides supersede general schedule and auto-expire

**Acceptance Criteria:**

Given a general schedule of Mon/Wed/Fri
When an override for next Tue/Thu 'Available, Daytime' is saved
Then overrides are persisted, displayed alongside general schedule, visible to Internal Users

Given an override with end date before start date
When submitted
Then validation error, override not saved

Given an override marking Wednesday 'Unavailable' while general schedule includes Wednesday
When Internal Users view availability
Then override takes precedence — Wednesday shows unavailable

Given expired overrides from last week
When auto-expire job runs
Then expired overrides removed from active list
adoFields.Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria:
Given general schedule Mon/Wed/Fri
When override Tue/Thu Daytime saved
Then persisted, displayed alongside schedule, visible to Internal Users

Given end date before start date
When submitted
Then validation error

Given override 'Unavailable' on Wednesday, general schedule includes Wednesday
When viewed
Then override takes precedence

Given expired overrides
When auto-expire job runs
Then removed from active list

adoFields.System.Title: Wave 4 — External Availability
adoFields.System.Description:
Delivers self-service availability management for External Users: recurring general schedule and one-time overrides. Entirely greenfield capability with no dependency on call-out/timecard features from Waves 2–3. Gated behind FF_CallOutManagement_Availability for independent rollout. Blocked by Wave 1 for feature flag infrastructure pattern.

**PRD user stories advanced:** #7 (general schedule), #8 (one-time overrides)

**Slices in this wave:**
- WI-007 (PBI, AFK): End-to-end general schedule availability management
- WI-008 (PBI, AFK): End-to-end one-time availability overrides (blocked by WI-007 — intra-wave dependency)

**Blocked by:** Wave 1 — Call-Out & Timecard Foundation (feature flag infrastructure pattern only).

adoFields.System.Title: Call-Out Management, Timecard Locking & External Availability
adoFields.System.Description: Extend MaxView's existing RTO capability into a broader call-out and attendance management system. This initiative adds structured call-out classification, automatic timecard-day locking for approved absences, paid-sick-time payout logic with accrual visibility, contract-position backfill shift creation, and self-service availability management for External Users.

assumptionsMade: Approved RTO/call-out means auto-processed — no explicit approval workflow exists today and building one is out of scope. 'Approved' in the context of timecard locking means the RTO request was successfully processed.
Partial-day absences (Late/Leave Early) lock the entire Timecard day, not just the affected time window. PRD Open Question #3 is resolved with full-day locking for simplicity.

The lead-time threshold (N hours) for RTO vs call-out classification is a worksite-level setting (WorksiteCallOutSetting.ThresholdHours), not a system-wide constant. Initial values are seeded via DB migration; admin UI is out of scope.

Accrual balance source is in RC (erecruit_Maxim); MaxView reads via a new MaxHub API endpoint with a session-scoped caching layer. The exact RC accrual tables need architectural review before WI-005 implementation (hence HITL classification).
Contract-position backfill shift attributes (start time, duration, department) are sourced from the original Match/Position metadata in RC, not from the call-out submission form.
Call-out notifications are sent to CandidateOwner + PositionOwner only; client/facility Contacts are not notified. PRD Open Question #6 is resolved with internal-only notification.
External availability is exclusively for External User HCPs. Internal Users have read-only access to view External User availability but cannot self-set their own.

Both parallel RTO submission paths (TimeClock Web via RtoOrchestrationService and MaxHub Mobile via AbsenceManagementOperation) must be extended for call-out type support. The MaxHub mobile email notification path (currently TODO in code) is wired as part of WI-003.
The >= boundary for ThresholdHours classification is inclusive for planned absence: a submission at exactly N hours before the shift is classified as planned, not as a call-out.
All call-out/timecard/accrual/backfill features share FF_CallOutManagement; external availability uses FF_CallOutManagement_Availability for independent rollout.
